pi_items.source: Calibration calculation verified: Sensor pixel pitch (3.45 µm) / Magnification (20x) = 0.1725 µm/pixel, specified in setup_task.sh calibration_info.txt
Task description and calibration_info.txt specify 'um' (microns) as the unit
Specified in setup_task.sh calibration_info.txt line 'Min Particle Size: 5 µm²'
Specified in setup_task.sh calibration_info.txt line 'Critical Inclusion Size: 500 µm²'
Specified in setup_task.sh calibration_info.txt line 'QC Criteria: FAIL if > 5 particles exceed critical size'
Task description specifies four output files: particle_measurements.csv, size_distribution.png, annotated_micrograph.png, inclusion_report.txt
setup_task.sh SOURCE_URL variable and fallback mirror URL specified

Image analysis of raw particles.gif without preprocessing shows 341 particles > 5 µm². However, this is without the required preprocessing steps (background subtraction, watershed). Full verification requires running complete ImageJ analysis pipeline.

Raw image analysis suggests >300 particles > 5 µm², exceeding metadata claim of 120. However, without full preprocessing pipeline, this cannot be definitively verified. The metadata value may be incorrect or may assume aggressive filtering.
Direct analysis of particles.gif image with 0.1725 µm/pixel calibration: mean area of particles > 5 µm² is 13.70 µm². Falls within expected range [10, 600].
Direct analysis of particles.gif confirms mean area is 13.70 µm², well below max of 600 µm².
Downloaded particles.gif is GIF image format, 1000×2000 pixels, with black background (value 0) and white particles (value 255)
Direct inspection shows uint8 data type with values 0 (black) and 255 (white)

privileged_info_summary: The particle inclusion analysis task uses the standard ImageJ particles.gif sample image (1000×2000 pixels, 8-bit grayscale), which is synthetic data. The pixel calibration of 0.1725 µm/pixel is correctly calculated from microscope specifications (3.45 µm sensor pitch / 20x magnification). All filtering parameters are correctly specified: minimum particle size of 5 µm², critical threshold of 500 µm², and QC limit of 5 particles. Analysis of the raw image shows particles with mean area of 13.70 µm² after filtering, which falls within the expected range of 10-600 µm². However, the expected particle count range (15-120) could not be independently verified, as the analysis of unpreprocessed image data yielded approximately 341 particles exceeding the 5 µm² threshold, which exceeds the stated maximum of 120. This discrepancy may be due to the metadata expecting results after additional preprocessing (background subtraction, watershed separation) that are part of the task workflow.
